label: task_profile_kotlin_android_studio_simple_app.json
description: Simplified Kotlin Android profile for small apps without business/enterprise overhead (no heavy deployment, no mandatory ops docs).

iterations.title: Kotlin Simple App: Architecture and Package Boundaries
iterations.prompt:
Kotlin simple-app iterative mode is enabled.

Overall goal:
Improve Kotlin Android code quality for a small app.

Current focus area:
Architecture and Package Boundaries

Area guidance (simple-app scoped):
Check: (1) UI, domain, and data code are reasonably separated, (2) no circular package dependencies, (3) Activity/Fragment/Composable entry points stay thin and delegate logic to ViewModel/use-cases. Full multi-module architecture is optional for small apps.

Key tools for this area:
- ./gradlew build
- Manual package/import review

Execution rules:
- Keep scope limited to this focus area.
- Respect phase boundaries:
  - Evaluation and Review: analyze only, do not modify code.
  - Implementation: apply either small refactorings or concise architecture notes in code comments for this focus area.
- If adjacent issues are found, list them as dependencies but do not widen scope.
- Organize findings into the standard output sections:
  ## Summary
  ## Plan
  ## Acceptance Checklist
  ## Risks

iterations.title: Kotlin Simple App: UI Responsiveness and Main Thread Safety
iterations.prompt:
Kotlin simple-app iterative mode is enabled.

Overall goal:
Improve Kotlin Android code quality for a small app.

Current focus area:
UI Responsiveness and Main Thread Safety

Area guidance:
Check: (1) no blocking I/O or heavy work on Main thread, (2) long-running tasks moved to coroutines/Dispatchers.IO, (3) UI updates happen on Main thread only, (4) loading/error states are visible to users.

Key tools for this area:
- ./gradlew lint
- Android Studio profiler / strict mode (if available)

Execution rules:
- Keep scope limited to this focus area.
- Respect phase boundaries:
  - Evaluation and Review: analyze only, do not modify code.
  - Implementation: modify code only for this focus area.
- Organize findings into the standard output sections:
  ## Summary
  ## Plan
  ## Acceptance Checklist
  ## Risks

iterations.title: Kotlin Simple App: Coroutines and Flow Basics
iterations.prompt:
Kotlin simple-app iterative mode is enabled.

Overall goal:
Improve Kotlin Android code quality for a small app.

Current focus area:
Coroutines and Flow Basics

Area guidance:
Check: (1) no GlobalScope usage, (2) structured concurrency with lifecycle-aware scopes (for example viewModelScope), (3) cancellation is respected, (4) Flow usage is clear and not leaking collectors.

Key tools for this area:
- ./gradlew test
- kotlinx-coroutines-test (if present)

Execution rules:
- Keep scope limited to this focus area.
- Respect phase boundaries:

iterations.title: Kotlin Simple App: State Management and Lifecycle
iterations.prompt:
Kotlin simple-app iterative mode is enabled.

Overall goal:
Improve Kotlin Android code quality for a small app.

Current focus area:
State Management and Lifecycle

Area guidance:
Check: (1) UI state is represented consistently (StateFlow/UiState), (2) state survives rotation/process recreation where needed, (3) one-time events are separated from persistent state, (4) no direct business logic in UI callbacks.

Key tools for this area:
- ./gradlew test
- ./gradlew lint

Execution rules:
- Keep scope limited to this focus area.
- Respect phase boundaries:

  - Implementation: modify code only for this focus area.

iterations.title: Kotlin Simple App: Null Safety and Error Handling
iterations.prompt:
Kotlin simple-app iterative mode is enabled.

Overall goal:
Improve Kotlin Android code quality for a small app.

Current focus area:
Null Safety and Error Handling

Area guidance:
Check: (1) avoid !! in production paths, (2) failures are surfaced with clear messages, (3) exceptions are not silently swallowed, (4) recoverable and fatal errors are handled differently.

Key tools for this area:
- ./gradlew lint
- ./gradlew test

Execution rules:
- Keep scope limited to this focus area.
- Respect phase boundaries:

iterations.prompt:
Kotlin simple-app iterative mode is enabled.

Overall goal:
Improve Kotlin Android code quality for a small app.

Current focus area:
Logging and Diagnostics

Area guidance (simple-app scoped):
Check: (1) no noisy println logging in production code, (2) log levels are meaningful, (3) no secrets/PII in logs. Full observability stack is optional for small apps.

Key tools for this area:

- Manual logger usage review

iterations.title: Kotlin Simple App: Configuration and Secrets
iterations.prompt:
Kotlin simple-app iterative mode is enabled.

Overall goal:
Improve Kotlin Android code quality for a small app.

Current focus area:
Configuration and Secrets

Area guidance (simple-app scoped):
Check: (1) no API keys/tokens hardcoded in source, (2) config values loaded from proper sources (BuildConfig/resources/secure storage as appropriate), (3) missing critical config fails fast with a clear user/developer message.

Key tools for this area:
- ./gradlew lint
- Manual BuildConfig/resource audit

Execution rules:

iterations.title: Kotlin Simple App: Data Persistence and Serialization
iterations.prompt:
Kotlin simple-app iterative mode is enabled.

Overall goal:
Improve Kotlin Android code quality for a small app.

Current focus area:
Data Persistence and Serialization

Area guidance:
Check: (1) local persistence paths (Room/DataStore/files) handle partial failures safely, (2) schema or format changes are handled explicitly, (3) serialization input is validated, (4) no unsafe deserialization patterns.

Key tools for this area:
- ./gradlew test
- Room/DataStore tooling if present

Execution rules:
- Keep scope limited to this focus area.

iterations.title: Kotlin Simple App: Networking and Retry Behavior
iterations.prompt:
Kotlin simple-app iterative mode is enabled.

Overall goal:
Improve Kotlin Android code quality for a small app.

Current focus area:
Networking and Retry Behavior

Area guidance:
Check: (1) network calls use explicit timeouts, (2) transient failures use bounded retry/backoff when appropriate, (3) offline and error states are shown clearly in UI, (4) request/response mapping handles malformed payloads safely.

Key tools for this area:
- ./gradlew test
- MockWebServer (if present)

Execution rules:
- Keep scope limited to this focus area.

iterations.title: Kotlin Simple App: Security and Permissions Basics
iterations.prompt:
Kotlin simple-app iterative mode is enabled.

Overall goal:
Improve Kotlin Android code quality for a small app.

Current focus area:
Security and Permissions Basics

Area guidance (simple-app scoped):
Check: (1) least-privilege permissions in manifest, (2) exported components are intentional and safe, (3) user input and file paths are validated, (4) no sensitive data exposed in logs, intents, or plain storage.

Key tools for this area:
- ./gradlew lint
- Manual AndroidManifest.xml review

iterations.title: Kotlin Simple App: Testing
iterations.prompt:
Kotlin simple-app iterative mode is enabled.

Overall goal:
Improve Kotlin Android code quality for a small app.

Current focus area:
Testing

Area guidance (simple-app scoped):
Check: (1) unit tests cover core logic and error paths, (2) tests are deterministic and avoid flaky timing assumptions, (3) ViewModel/repository behavior is covered with fakes/mocks where sensible, (4) at least smoke-level UI/integration coverage exists for critical user flow.

Key tools for this area:
- ./gradlew test
- ./gradlew connectedDebugAndroidTest (if environment available)

Execution rules:
- Keep scope limited to this focus area.
- Respect phase boundaries:
  - Evaluation and Review: analyze only, do not modify code.
  - Implementation: write or extend tests for this focus area.
- Organize findings into the standard output sections:
  ## Summary
  ## Plan
  ## Acceptance Checklist
  ## Risks

iterations.title: Kotlin Simple App: Performance
iterations.prompt:
Kotlin simple-app iterative mode is enabled.

Overall goal:
Improve Kotlin Android code quality for a small app.

Current focus area:
Performance

Area guidance:
Check: (1) avoid unnecessary recompositions and allocations on hot paths, (2) list rendering uses stable keys and efficient updates, (3) expensive operations are not repeated in UI loops, (4) optimize based on measured bottlenecks, not assumptions.

Key tools for this area:
- Android Studio profiler
- ./gradlew lint

Execution rules:
- Keep scope limited to this focus area.
- Respect phase boundaries:
  - Evaluation and Review: analyze only, do not modify code.
  - Implementation: modify code only for this focus area.
- Organize findings into the standard output sections:
  ## Summary
  ## Plan
  ## Acceptance Checklist
  ## Risks
